meaning: adj.在前部的,以前的adv.在前面,在船头n.前部,船头 prep.在前
examples: She has always been to the fore at moments of crisis.
The problem has come to the fore again in recent months.
There had been no direct damage in the fore part of the ship.
If you can't score a spot at Bresca, try Fore St, which is arguably just as good.
This is where the frontier aspect of doing business in China comes to the fore.
